summary: Functional foundations are a core topic group in the modern Java study path.
Stream processing is a core topic group in the modern Java study path.
Null-safety, time modeling, and interface evolution are grouped as safer Java boundary topics.
Async and reactive foundations extend the Java backend study path beyond synchronous request processing.
Behavior parameterization belongs to the functional foundation of modern Java.
Lambda expressions implement behavior parameterization through concise behavior values.
Lambda expressions are typed through functional interface target types.
The Stream API is the primary component for declarative collection pipelines.
Stream operations commonly receive lambdas or method references as behavioral parameters.
Collectors terminate or aggregate stream pipelines into structured results.
Stream pipelines should avoid hidden side effects and shared mutable state unless mutation is intentionally isolated.
Parallel stream use should be treated as a measured performance decision rather than a default refactor.
Optional belongs to the null-safety part of the modern Java study path.
Modern date/time types belong to backend boundary modeling for temporal values.
Default methods belong to interface evolution topics.
CompletableFuture is a key Java async abstraction in the async/reactive topic group.
Backend style uses CompletableFuture only when async ownership, composition, and error handling are explicit.
Backend style uses Optional at absence boundaries while keeping DTO and persistence conventions explicit.
Backend style uses modern date/time types to avoid ambiguous temporal values in domain and API contracts.
Backend style uses stream pipelines for clear transformations while keeping business effects explicit.
Modern Java backend style must avoid hiding business effects inside stream operations.
Modern Java backend style treats parallel stream adoption as a benchmarked change with concurrency review.
Functional foundations prepare the reader for stream processing.
Stream processing and side-effect discipline prepare the reader for async and reactive composition.
